title: BigBear.ai Announces First Quarter 2026 Results, Increases Backlog with National Security Contracts
text: BigBear.ai reported Q1 2026 revenue of $34.4 million, down 1% year-over-year, though gross margins expanded significantly to 34.0% from 21.3% in the prior year. The company posted a net loss of $56.8 million compared to $62.0 million in Q1 2025. The company secured approximately $75 million in new contracts during the quarter with backlog increased with over $60 million in national security-focused awards. BigBear.ai redeemed $124.6 million in convertible notes, reducing debt obligations. Only $16.5 million in debt remains outstanding (due end of 2026). The company implemented an internal restructuring to align sales, technology, delivery, and customer success teams with growth priorities. The Ask Sage acquisition, completed in Q4 2025, contributed higher-margin generative AI revenue during the quarter. CEO Kevin McAleenan stated: These wins keep us on track to meet our topline revenue target for 2026 and we are pleased to see our pipeline strengthening. The organization remains focused on national security and trade & travel markets as primary growth drivers.
